chunks.text:
# Concept: Customer
Id: concept:customer
Synonyms: user, client, buyer, account holder
Links: table:public.users
A person who has signed up and placed at least one order.

# Concept: Revenue
Id: concept:revenue
Synonyms: sales, gross sales, top line
Links: table:public.orders#total_amount
Sum of `orders.total_amount` where `status = 'paid'`, expressed in cents.

# public.orders
Customer purchase orders. One row per submitted order.
Aliases: purchases, sales, transactions
Primary entity: order
Relationships:
- table:public.orders#user_id -> table:public.users#id
Columns:
- id uuid (PK NOT NULL)
- user_id uuid (NOT NULL)
- status text (NOT NULL) — Order lifecycle state. Most reporting filters on `paid`.
- total_amount integer (NOT NULL) — Stored in cents. Use `total_amount / 100.0` for dollar values.

# public.orders — business context
Orders move through `pending → paid → shipped` and may end in `cancelled`. The `total_amount` column is stored in cents.

# public.orders (also: purchases, sales, transactions) — common query language
- "sales" usually means paid orders (`status = 'paid'`)
- "revenue" usually means `sum(total_amount)` where `status = 'paid'`
- "new orders" usually means orders created in the selected date range

Column: public.orders.id uuid (PK NOT NULL)
Id: table:public.orders#id
Aliases: order_id

# public.orders [order] — example question
How much revenue did we make last month?

# public.orders [order] — example question
Which customers placed the most orders?

# public.orders [order] — example question
How many orders were cancelled this week?

Column: public.orders.status text (NOT NULL)
Id: table:public.orders#status
Order lifecycle state. Most reporting filters on `paid`.
Aliases: order_status
Values: pending, paid, shipped, cancelled

Column: public.orders.total_amount integer (NOT NULL)
Id: table:public.orders#total_amount
Stored in cents. Use `total_amount / 100.0` for dollar values.
Aliases: revenue, sale_amount

Column: public.orders.user_id uuid (NOT NULL)
Id: table:public.orders#user_id

# public.users
Registered user accounts. One row per signed-up user.
Aliases: accounts, members
Primary entity: user
Columns:
- id uuid (PK NOT NULL)
- created_at timestamptz (NOT NULL) — When the user account was created.

# public.users (also: accounts, members) — common query language
- "users" and "members" refer to this table
- "new users" usually means accounts created in the selected date range
- "active users" typically means users who placed at least one order

Column: public.users.created_at timestamptz (NOT NULL)
Id: table:public.users#created_at
When the user account was created.
Aliases: signup_date, registered_at

Column: public.users.id uuid (PK NOT NULL)
Id: table:public.users#id
Aliases: user_id

# public.users [user] — example question
How many users signed up last month?

# public.users [user] — example question
Which users placed the most orders?
